steps.title: Categorize
steps.description: Categorize the information system and the information processed, stored, and transmitted by that system based on an impact analysis (FIPS 199/CNSSI 1253).
activities: Identify the information types processed, stored, or transmitted
Determine the impact level (Low, Moderate, High) for each security objective (CIA)
Assign the overall system categorization based on the highest impact level
Register the system in eMASS or equivalent authorization tool
Document the categorization in the System Security Plan (SSP)

steps.title: Select
steps.description: Select the appropriate set of security controls for the system based on its categorization and apply tailoring guidance.
activities: Identify the initial baseline controls from NIST SP 800-53
Apply tailoring guidance to remove non-applicable controls
Add supplemental controls as required by DoD or DISA
Identify common controls provided by the infrastructure or cloud provider
Document control selections and rationale in the SSP

steps.title: Implement
steps.description: Implement the selected security controls and document how the controls are deployed within the system and its operating environment.
activities: Deploy technical, operational, and management security controls
Configure systems per DISA STIGs and SRGs
Document control implementation details in the SSP
Develop and document standard operating procedures (SOPs)
Implement continuous monitoring capabilities

steps.title: Assess
steps.description: Assess the security controls to determine the extent to which they are implemented correctly, operating as intended, and producing the desired outcome.
activities: Develop the Security Assessment Plan (SAP)
Conduct security control assessments per NIST SP 800-53A
Run automated scanning tools (ACAS, SCAP, STIG Viewer)
Document findings in the Security Assessment Report (SAR)
Develop the Plan of Action and Milestones (POA&M) for findings

steps.title: Authorize
steps.description: Authorize the system based on a determination that the risk to organizational operations, assets, and individuals is acceptable.
activities: Compile the authorization package (SSP, SAR, POA&M)
Present residual risks to the Authorizing Official (AO)
AO makes risk-based authorization decision (ATO, DATO, IATO)
Issue the authorization decision letter
Set authorization boundary and terms/conditions

steps.title: Monitor
steps.description: Monitor security controls on an ongoing basis to ensure continued effectiveness and to support risk management decisions.
activities: Implement continuous monitoring strategy per NIST SP 800-137
Conduct ongoing security control assessments
Monitor and analyze security alerts and vulnerabilities
Report security posture to AO and stakeholders
Update SSP, SAR, and POA&M as changes occur

navy.description: Navy RMF authorization follows SECNAVINST 5239.3C with Navy-specific requirements and SPAWAR/NAVCYBERFOR oversight.
requirements: Register system in eMASS with Navy-specific categorization
Submit authorization package to Navy AO via chain of command
navy.timeline: Typical Navy ATO timeline: 6-12 months depending on system complexity and impact level.

army.description: Army RMF authorization follows AR 25-2 with Army-specific cyber requirements and ARCYBER oversight.
requirements: Register in eMASS with Army POC chain
Follow ARCYBER directive for STIG compliance
Coordinate with ARCYBER for interconnection agreements
army.timeline: Typical Army ATO timeline: 4-8 months for tactical systems, 6-12 months for enterprise.

airforce.description: Air Force RMF authorization follows AFI 17-130 with 16th Air Force/CYBERCOM oversight.
requirements: Register in eMASS with AF-specific attributes
Submit authorization package to AF AO
airforce.timeline: Typical Air Force ATO timeline: 6-9 months.
